title: CT HCC Screening
templateData:
EXAMINATION: [CT abdomen with contrast.]

CLINICAL INFORMATION: [Hepatic cirrhosis. Surveillance for hepatocellular carcinoma.]

COMPARISON:  [None.]

TECHNIQUE:  
  Axial 2.5 mm images of the abdomen; sagittal and coronal reformatted images.
  IV contrast:  [Omnipaque-350]  [125] mL.
  Oral contrast:  [Water was given as enteric contrast.]
  Technical comments:  [Dual-phase with equilibrium imaging of the abdomen.]

FINDINGS:

HEPATOBILIARY SYSTEM
Liver morphology:  [Cirrhosis evidenced by heterogeneous appearance, surface nodularity, and/or hypertrophy of the left/caudate lobes.]

Portal hypertension: [Described the presence/absence of portal hypertension. If present, described the portosystemic varices.]
   Spleen:  [Splenomegaly.]    [  ]  cm.        
   Ascites:  [None.]

Focal liver lesions:
   Lesions compatible with hepatocellular carcinoma by OPTN class 5 criteria:  [Dictate "no" or "Powerscribe hepatocellular carcinoma"]

   Indeterminate liver lesions:  [None.]

   Benign liver lesions:   [None.]

Hepatic vasculature:    
   Portal and hepatic veins:  [Normally patent.]
   Arterial anatomy:  [Normal (Type I).]

Gallbladder and bile ducts:
   Gallbladder:  [Gallbladder in situ. No gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid.]
   Bile ducts:   [No intra-or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.] 
   
RETROPERITONEUM
   Pancreas:  [Normal.]
   Adrenals:  [Normal.]
   Kidneys:  [Normal.]
   Lymph nodes:  [No lymphadenopathy in the abdomen or pelvis.]

GASTROINTESTINAL
  [Imaged bowel and mesentery are normal.]

LOWER CHEST  
   [Heart is normal in size. Lung bases are clear. No pleural or pericardial effusions.]

BONES
  [ No significant lesion.]


Impression:
[Impression statements here]


The following OPTN class 5 criteria are used to determine if liver lesions are compatible with hepatocellular carcinoma by imaging, as follows:

OPTN Class 5A - Must meet ALL criteria:
   1. Nodule measuring between 1 and 1.9 cm.
   2. Increased contrast enhancement on late arterial phase ("hypervascularity"), relative to hepatic parenchyma.
   3. BOTH "washout" on later contrast phase AND delayed peripheral rim enhancement (capsule/pseudocapsule).

OPTN Class 5A-g - Must meet ALL criteria:
   1. Nodule measuring between 1 and 1.9 cm.
   2. Increased contrast enhancement on late arterial phase (hypervascularity relative to hepatic parenchyma).
   3. Growth (maximum diameter) by 50% or more on serial MR/CT obtained at least 6 months apart. Growth criteria does not apply to previously ablated lesions.

OPTN Class 5B - Must meet ALL criteria:
   1. Nodule measuring between 2 and 5 cm.
   2. Increased contrast enhancement on late arterial phase ("hypervascularity"), relative to hepatic parenchyma.
   3. One of the following:
       a. "Washout" on portal venous/delayed phase.
       b. Delayed peripheral rim enhancement (capsule/pseudocapsule).
       c. Growth (maximum diameter) by 50% or more on serial MR/CT obtained at least 6 months apart. Growth criteria does not apply to previously ablated lesions
       d. Biopsy proven.

http://optn.transplant.hrsa.gov/resources/by-organ/liver-intestine/



Milan Criteria for potential transplantation:
   -  one lesion smaller than 5 cm 
   -  up to 3 lesions smaller than 3 cm
   -  no extrahepatic manifestations 
   -  no vascular invasion

“Hepatocellular Carcinoma” sub-template

Lesion # [  ]  :
   Lesion size: [  ]  cm (series  [ ]   , image [ ]   ).
   Lesion location: Segment  [  ] .

   Hypervascularity:  [Yes/no]
   Washout:  [Yes/no]
   Capsule/pseudocapsule:  [Yes/no].

   Change in lesion since prior exam:  [Not applicable. Baseline study.]
